beats.activity: Wake up and make pour-over coffee at home, quiet morning ritual.
beats.reasoning: Logan's sacred morning routine—those quiet moments with coffee before the day starts are non-negotiable and set his tone. Starts the day grounded.

beats.activity: Commute to the coffee shop in Interbay.
beats.reasoning: Saturday shift at the shop; overcast weather is mild for the drive. Budget 35 min for Northgate to Interbay across town.

beats.activity: Work shift: arrive early, prep bar, handle Saturday morning rush, manage team.
beats.reasoning: Saturday mornings are consistently busy at third-wave shops; Logan arrives early to set the tone and lead the team through the rush. Four hours covers setup, peak service, and early lunch lull.

beats.activity: Lunch break: simple sandwich or salad, eat at the shop or nearby, recharge.
beats.reasoning: Logan stays local during his break—frugal and consistent with his habit of eating in the shade and watching the world pass by. Gives him 45 min to rest before the afternoon push.

beats.activity: Work shift continues: afternoon service, train staff, engage with regulars.
beats.reasoning: Saturday afternoon typically mellows out around 3–4pm, giving Logan space to mentor and connect with customers—the parts of the job he values most.
After shift, Logan heads to one of Seattle's active farmers markets (Ballard is in full swing Saturday afternoon). 30 min allows for Interbay-to-Ballard drive and parking.

beats.activity: Browse and shoot photos at Ballard Farmers Market with film camera, pick up fresh produce.
beats.reasoning: Logan brings his film camera everywhere and loves light and crowds. Overcast Saturday is perfect for even, soft light. Farmers markets offer produce shopping and people-watching without the intensity of Pride; frugal and low-key.

beats.activity: Drive home to Northgate with farmers market haul.
beats.reasoning: Evening commute back across town after market. 40 min budget for Ballard to Northgate drive with parking.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat a simple dinner using fresh farmers market ingredients.
beats.reasoning: Logan is frugal and values quiet evenings at home. Cooking with fresh market produce aligns with his sustainable, intentional lifestyle.

beats.activity: Sort through film contact sheets, listen to lo-fi music, wind down for the night.
beats.reasoning: Logan's evening ritual mirrors Day -3: reflecting on the day's photos, low-key music. Saturday evening at home feels natural after a full work shift and farmers market outing. Prepares him for sleep.
Full night of sleep (7.5 hours) ending well before 06:00 cutoff. Logan ends his Saturday settled and grounded, ready for Sunday.

diary: Started the day right with my pour-over at home—that quiet ritual before the world gets loud. The commute to Interbay was smooth, overcast but not dreary. Saturday morning rush hit hard around 9:30, the usual chaos of people wanting their weekend caffeine fix. I caught myself smiling at this regular, Marcus, who always orders a cortado and asks about my photography. Lunch was just a quick sandwich between waves. By the time I clocked out, my feet were sore but my head was clear. Drove over to Ballard with the farmers market calling to me. Shot some good frames with my film camera—the light was soft and strange through the clouds. Grabbed fresh greens and heirloom tomatoes. Made a simple dinner at home, nothing fancy, just good ingredients. Spent the evening sorting through contact sheets with lo-fi playing. There's something grounding about a Saturday that moves between service and solitude.
thought_process: I was in a good headspace this morning—the ritual coffee helped. Work felt purposeful rather than draining, maybe because I focused on the team and regulars instead of just pushing through tickets. I almost skipped the farmers market, thinking I'd just grab groceries, but the overcast weather actually drew me out. Photography grounds me when the shift work gets repetitive. The drive home felt like a natural wind-down. I think I needed that transition time between the shop and my own space. By evening, sorting film and listening to music felt like the right pace—nothing forced, just letting the day settle.
world_event_prompt: Overcast Saturday, high of 64F. Farmers markets in Ballard, Fremont, and the U-District in full swing. Mariners home game at T-Mobile Park at 1pm brings SoDo crowds.
